VERA
Visual Evaluation · Reporting · Analytics

Evaluate anything.
Prove everything.

Structured jury scoring for exhibitions, competitions, and review panels. Configurable criteria, real-time data capture, and outcome reports your accreditation body trusts.

Interactive demo with real evaluation data — no sign-up required.

How it works
1

Set Up & Share

Define criteria, add projects, invite jurors. Share a QR code or link — evaluators join in seconds.

2

Score Live

Jurors evaluate on any device. Scores auto-save on every input — no paper forms, no data entry.

3

Report & Prove

Rankings, outcome attainment, analytics, and exports — accreditation-ready the moment scoring ends.

Why teams choose VERA

Flexible Criteria

Define rubrics per evaluation period — technical, design, delivery, or any domain-specific criteria. Map each to programme outcomes.

Real-Time Scoring

Jurors score on any device during the event. Auto-save on every input, live sync, PIN-secured sessions. Zero friction.

Outcome-Level Reporting

Every score maps to programme outcomes. Generate accreditation-ready attainment reports — not just rankings.

The difference

Without VERA

✗ Paper forms collected manually at the event
✗ Scores entered into spreadsheets after the fact
✗ Outcome mapping done by hand for accreditation
✗ No audit trail — impossible to verify or defend
✗ Results take days to compile and distribute
→

With VERA

✓ Jurors score on their phones in real time
✓ Every input auto-saved — zero data entry
✓ Scores auto-map to programme outcomes
✓ Full audit trail with action-level history
✓ Rankings and reports ready the moment scoring ends
The juror experience

No app to install, no training needed. Jurors scan a QR code, enter their name, and start scoring.

"

We evaluated 41 capstone projects with 19 jurors in under two hours. Scores were live, rankings were instant, and the accreditation report was ready before we left the building.

AY
Prof. Ahmet Yılmaz
EE Department · Poster Day Coordinator
Works on any device
PIN-secured sessions
XLSX & PDF export
Real-time analytics
Accreditation-ready
Common questions
Is VERA free to use?
VERA is free for academic departments and research groups. For large-scale commercial use or custom deployments, contact us for pricing.
Can I define my own evaluation criteria?
Yes. Each evaluation period has fully configurable criteria — labels, weights, max scores, rubric descriptions, and programme outcome mappings. You can use the built-in defaults or create domain-specific criteria from scratch.
Which accreditation standards does VERA support?
VERA supports MÜDEK and ABET programme outcome mapping out of the box. You can also define custom outcome frameworks for any accreditation body or internal quality standard.
Do jurors need to install an app?
No. VERA runs entirely in the browser. Jurors scan a QR code or click a link, enter their name, and start scoring. No downloads, no accounts, no training.
What happens if a juror loses connection?
Scores auto-save after every input. If disconnected, the juror can return using their 4-digit session PIN and resume exactly where they left off. No data is lost.
Where is evaluation data stored?
All data is stored in a secure Supabase (PostgreSQL) database with row-level security, role-based access controls, and a full audit trail. Each organization's data is completely isolated.
The admin experience

Everything admins need to run evaluations, manage teams, and generate reports — in one dashboard.

Excel (.xlsx) · Score Rankings
8 projects · 9 jurors · Summer 2026
#Project▲Team MembersTechnical▲Design▲Delivery▲Teamwork▲Average▼
Consensus▲?
Juror Consensus

Measures how much jurors agree on a project's score. Based on the standard deviation (σ) of total scores across all jurors.

Highσ < 3.0 — Jurors closely agree
Moderateσ 3.0–5.0 — Some variation
Disputedσ > 5.0 — Significant disagreement

Hover each badge to see the exact σ value and score range.

Programme Outcome Analytics
Outcome attainment & continuous improvement evidence — Summer 2026
MÜDEK 2024 · 8 outcomes mapped
Active Framework: MÜDEK 2024
This dashboard provides direct assessment evidence for the following programme outcomes from the active accreditation framework:
1.2Knowledge application to complex problems
2Problem identification & analysis
3.1Creative engineering solutions
3.2Design under realistic constraints
8.1Intra-disciplinary teamwork
8.2Multi-disciplinary teamwork
9.1Oral communication
9.2Written communication
Attainment target: ≥70% of evaluations must meet the scoring threshold

Accreditation frameworks require longitudinal evidence of outcome monitoring ("closing the loop"). Only evaluation periods sharing the same criteria template are compared.

≥80% (Excellent)
70–79% (Met)
60–69% (Borderline)
<60% (Below target)
Verifies programme-level claims at individual group level — a high mean can mask weak groups if distribution is skewed.
07Assessment Reliability
Juror Consistency Heatmap
Coefficient of Variation (CV = σ/μ × 100) per group × criterion — measures inter-rater agreement
Criterion SDR Receiver Buck Conv. EEG Board Line Robot IoT Greenhouse FPGA Edge Sensor Node Class-D Amp
Technical 6.2% 8.4% 9.1% 12.3% 16.8% 11.5% 19.2% 22.1%
Written 5.8% 7.1% 8.3% 9.7% 11.4% 17.6% 13.2% 15.9%
Oral 7.4% 9.8% 12.6% 14.1% 18.3% 21.5% 26.7% 28.4%
Teamwork 8.9% 12.7% 16.4% 15.3% 20.8% 25.1% 27.9% 31.2%
<10% CV (Excellent agreement)
10–15% CV (Good)
15–25% CV (Moderate)
>25% CV (Poor — review needed)
CV >25% indicates poor inter-rater agreement — undermines attainment claims. Consider juror calibration or rubric clarification.
08Assessment Instrument Coverage
Outcome × Assessment Tool Matrix
Shows which programme outcomes are directly measured by VERA’s jury evaluation criteria (4 of 18 codes)
Coverage levels: DirectCriterion explicitly maps to this outcome IndirectAssessed tangentially, no explicit mapping Not mappedMay need a separate instrument
Outcome Technical Written Oral Teamwork Coverage
1.1 Mathematics & science knowledge — — — — Not mapped
1.2 Apply knowledge to complex problems ✓ Direct — — — Direct
2 Problem identification & analysis ✓ Direct — — — Direct
3.1 Creative engineering solutions ✓ Direct — — — Direct
3.2 Design under realistic constraints ✓ Direct — — — Direct
4 Modern engineering tools ~ Indirect — — — Indirect
5 Research methods ~ Indirect — — — Indirect
6.1 Societal impact awareness — — — — Not mapped
6.2 Legal consequences awareness — — — — Not mapped
7.1 Professional & ethical conduct — — — ~ Indirect Indirect
7.2 Non-discrimination & inclusivity — — — — Not mapped
8.1 Intra-disciplinary teamwork — — — ✓ Direct Direct
8.2 Multi-disciplinary teamwork — — — ✓ Direct Direct
9.1 Oral communication — — ✓ Direct — Direct
9.2 Written communication — ✓ Direct — — Direct
10.1 Project management — — — — Not mapped
10.2 Entrepreneurship — — — — Not mapped
11 Lifelong learning — — — — Not mapped
8 Direct assessment
3 Indirect evidence
7 Not mapped — requires other instruments
Accreditation frameworks require each outcome to have at least one direct instrument. Unmapped outcomes need additional tools (exams, lab reports, surveys).

Code Outcome Mapped Criteria
Coverage?
Coverage Levels

Indicates how each programme outcome relates to your evaluation criteria.

DirectOne or more criteria explicitly assess this outcome. Strongest coverage for accreditation.
IndirectAssessed tangentially through related criteria without an explicit mapping. Valid but weaker evidence.
Not mappedNo criterion covers this outcome. May require a supplementary assessment instrument.

Design Quality
30 pts
Label
Short
Weight
Locked — scores exist
1.1 4 5 · 4 bands
MÜDEK 2024
3.1
User needs and scenario framing
Shows a clear grasp of audience, context, and the problem the design must solve.
3.2
Design alternatives and tradeoffs
Compares options thoughtfully and explains why one direction is preferred.
4.1
Interface and system coherence
Maintains a coherent information structure, visual hierarchy, and component behavior.
4.0 - 5.0 Exemplary
Polished and intentional
Strong layout decisions, cohesive visual language, and no distracting inconsistencies.
3.0 - 3.9 Strong
Clear and usable
Structure is sound and readable, with minor gaps in spacing, alignment, or hierarchy.
2.0 - 2.9 Adequate
Functional but uneven
Gets the idea across, but visual consistency and polish need more attention.
0.0 - 1.9 Needs work
Confusing or incomplete
Layout or interaction cues are inconsistent enough to reduce confidence and clarity.
Delivery
30 pts
Label
Short
Weight
Locked — scores exist
6 7 · 4 bands
MÜDEK 2024
2.2
Project planning and milestone discipline
Reflects a delivery plan with milestones, dependencies, and realistic execution.
4.2
Integration and delivery readiness
Shows reliable handoff quality with stable files, clean naming, and reproducible delivery.
4.0 - 5.0 Exemplary
On-time and production-ready
Delivered on schedule with clean assets, stable scope, and no avoidable rework.
3.0 - 3.9 Strong
Mostly reliable
Meets the delivery target with minor timing or packaging issues that do not block review.
2.0 - 2.9 Adequate
Late or uneven
Submission is usable, but the handoff feels rushed, incomplete, or lightly documented.
0.0 - 1.9 Needs work
Hard to evaluate
Missing deliverables, broken files, or unclear packaging prevents confident assessment.
Teamwork
10 pts
Label
Short
Weight
Locked — scores exist
8 9 · 4 bands
MÜDEK 2024
3.3
Collaboration and ownership
Shows shared responsibility, active contribution, and clear role ownership within the team.
5.2
Communication and professionalism
Communicates progress cleanly, resolves issues early, and keeps documentation review-friendly.
4.0 - 5.0 Exemplary
Highly coordinated
Team contributions are balanced, communication is proactive, and handoffs are effortless.
3.0 - 3.9 Strong
Good team rhythm
Members contribute consistently, with small coordination or communication gaps.
2.0 - 2.9 Adequate
Uneven participation
Participation exists, but roles or communication are not yet fully stable.
0.0 - 1.9 Needs work
Low coordination
Team process is unclear, with weak coordination or repeated ownership gaps.
4 criteria · 100 pts

Disable Organization
This action blocks all access for the organization.
All organization members will lose access
Admins and jurors won't be able to log in or access evaluation data. Active jury sessions will be terminated.
This action can be reversed by re-enabling the organization.
Revoke Admin Access
Remove an admin's access across all organizations.
Irreversible action
All memberships will be removed. The admin must re-apply through the application flow to regain access.
Start Maintenance Mode
Immediately block all non-super-admin access.
All users will be locked out immediately
Active jury sessions will be interrupted. Org admins will see a maintenance screen. Only super admins retain access.
